metadata_update.root_cause: NOT a 31B model defect. Ollama 0.32.1 sets thinking=true as default for the gemma4 parser. The model generates 'thinking' tokens (chain-of-thought) before producing the visible answer. These thinking tokens are hidden from the /api/generate `response` field by default. With small num_predict (e.g., 20), the entire budget is consumed by thinking, yielding response='' with done_reason='length'. The same bug exists identically on gemma4:8B, gemma4:26b, and gemma4:31b. Confirmed via Ollama issue #16562 (gemma4:12b same symptom) and PR #16850 (proposed server-side default-think=false fix, still draft).
metadata_update.recommended_fix: Apply caller-side fix: add `"think": false` to all /api/generate and /api/chat request bodies when using gemma4 family models. Alternative for CLI: `ollama run --think=false`. Do NOT re-pull, re-quantize, or upgrade/downgrade Ollama — the model is functional. The 19GB gemma4:31b model file is preserved; no need to delete.

production_readiness_post_fix.gemma4_31b: GO-WITH-CAVEATS (memory pressure on 48GB; suitable for short/medium context only)

next_actions: Yoda: Update TKT-1016 status from in_progress to pending_review with the investigation report
Yoda: Add 'use think: false for gemma4 family' guidance to model-routing skill
Yoda: Search OpenClaw codebase for any /api/generate calls against gemma4:* and ensure they include think: false (or document why thinking is wanted)
Optional: Re-run TKT-1015 capacity test with think=false to get true 31B throughput numbers (expected: similar or higher TPS due to fewer tokens generated)

key_metrics.reproductions: 20+ empty-response cases confirmed across 3 model sizes and 5 prompt types
